mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals impending economic contraction, reducing corporate profit expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate indicate an aggressive monetary tightening response to price pressures, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fail to predict negative returns with a rank IC above 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly increase in the industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which directly boosts earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative mean forward returns during recovery phases, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales of new and used motor vehicles reflect strong consumer purchasing power and durable goods demand, which translates into immediate positive sentiment and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns with a win rate above 55 percent in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.5 million units annually, construction sector contraction signals weakening aggregate demand and tighter credit conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of months where HOUST drops below 1500, invalidating the recessionary signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates indicate robust labor market expansion without overheating, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and stable UNRATE produces negative forward returns with a rank IC below negative 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces gross domestic product growth, supply-side efficiency gains reduce input costs and expand profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO outperformance relative to GDPC1 fails to predict positive returns with a win rate above 58 percent in out-of-sample periods.
